audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's answer to Bildad: social alienation, God as opponent imagery, plea for pity, desire for words inscribed, goel/vindicator lives, and warning to friends. Goel language is preserved as vindicator/redeemer without forcing later doctrinal readings.

verses.restored: Job answered and said:
How long will you torment my soul and crush me with words?
These ten times you have insulted me; you are not ashamed to wrong me.
Even if I truly have erred, my error lodges with me.
If indeed you magnify yourselves against me and prove my disgrace against me,
know then that the Divine has bent me and surrounded me with his net.
Look, I cry out, Violence, but I am not answered; I call for help, but there is no justice.
He has walled up my way so I cannot pass, and he has set darkness on my paths.
He has stripped my glory from me and removed the crown from my head.
He breaks me down on every side, and I go; he uproots my hope like a tree.
He has kindled his anger against me and counted me as his adversary.
His troops come together, build up their way against me, and camp around my tent.
My brothers he has put far from me, and those who know me are wholly estranged from me.
My relatives have failed, and my close companions have forgotten me.
Guests in my house and my slave-women count me a stranger; I have become a foreigner in their eyes.
I call to my servant, but he does not answer; with my mouth I plead to him.
My breath is strange to my woman, and my appeal to the children of my mother’s womb.
Even young children reject me; when I rise, they speak against me.
All my intimate companions abhor me, and those I loved have turned against me.
My bone clings to my skin and flesh, and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.
Have pity on me, have pity on me, you my friends, for the hand of the Divine has touched me.
Why do you pursue me like the Divine and are not satisfied with my flesh?
If only my words were written! If only they were inscribed in a scroll!
With an iron stylus and lead, engraved in rock forever!
But I myself know that my vindicator/redeemer lives, and at the last he will stand upon the dust.
After my skin has been thus destroyed, yet from my flesh I shall see the Divine,
whom I myself shall see for me; my eyes shall behold, and not a stranger. My kidneys are consumed within me.
If you say, How shall we pursue him? and, The root of the matter is found in him,
fear the sword for yourselves, for wrath brings punishments of the sword, so that you may know there is judgment.
